name: Woodland Hills Pawn Shop by Maxferd
description_short: Maxferd Jewelry and Loan operates a pawn shop in Woodland Hills, CA offering collateral-based loans, jewelry sales, appraisals, and precious metals buying since 1884.
description_long: Maxferd Jewelry and Loan traces its roots to 1884, positioning itself as an established player in the pawn industry with over a century of operational history. The Woodland Hills location serves the Los Angeles community with a focus on jewelry, watches, precious metals, and collectibles. The company emphasizes reliability, fair pricing, and client confidentiality as core principles inherited from its founding mission. The pawn shop operates from a physical storefront at 20930 Ventura Boulevard in Woodland Hills, serving the San Fernando Valley area. Maxferd offers a suite of services centered on collateral-based lending, jewelry evaluation, and asset purchasing. Their primary loan product targets customers seeking short-term cash against personal items like jewelry and watches, positioned as faster and cheaper than credit cards or bank loans. The company also provides jewelry repair, appraisals, gold buying, precious metals trading, and an online store for customers to purchase items. Their marketing emphasizes accurate market-based valuations performed by certified appraisers, transparent transaction processes, and professional customer service. The shop distinguishes itself through claimed expertise in market trends, a wide range of accepted collateral items (jewelry, watches, electronics, collectibles, musical instruments), and active community engagement. Maxferd advertises a promotional offer of 0% interest loans on single loans under $2,500 for new clients. The honest assessment is that this is a legitimate pawn operation suitable for customers needing immediate cash against jewelry or valuables, though like all pawn transactions, customers should understand that items serve as collateral and may be sold if loans aren't repaid. The company's century-old history suggests stability, but customers should independently verify current appraisal fairness and loan terms.
pros: Established 1884 heritage suggests operational stability and long-term business credibility
0% interest promotion on loans under $2,500 for new clients reduces borrowing costs
Team of certified appraisers claimed to provide accurate market-based valuations
Fast collateral-based loans positioned as quicker than bank loans with minimal documentation
Wide range of accepted items including jewelry, watches, electronics, collectibles, and musical instruments
Convenient Woodland Hills location at corner of Ventura and De Soto with extended weekday hours
Offers multiple revenue streams including loan origination, jewelry repair, appraisals, and retail sales
cons: Limited transparency on actual loan terms, APR rates, and repayment timelines in provided website content
Pawn model inherently risks item loss if borrower cannot repay loan plus accrued interest and fees
Store hours limited to 10 AM-5:45 PM weekdays and 10 AM-4:30 PM Saturday with no Sunday service
Website contains excessive keyword stuffing and minimal substantive service details, reducing trustworthiness
No published pricing examples, fee schedules, or loan term matrices to help customers understand actual costs
best_for: Individuals needing immediate emergency cash against jewelry, watches, or valuable collectibles
People seeking short-term loans without credit checks or bank qualification requirements
Sellers of gold, precious metals, or luxury items wanting professional appraisals and fair market pricing
Customers seeking jewelry repair or appraisal services in the Woodland Hills/San Fernando Valley area
diagnosis: Maxferd Woodland Hills is best for customers with valuable personal items who need immediate cash and prefer collateral-based lending over credit products. The main caveat is that pawn loans are short-term products secured by personal property—failure to repay results in item loss—and the website lacks transparent disclosure of specific interest rates, fees, and repayment terms needed to evaluate true costs.
services: Collateral-based pawn loans on jewelry and watches
Professional appraisals and valuations of jewelry and precious items
Musical instruments collateral loans and sales
Online retail store for purchasing pawn inventory
